title: Machine Learning Prediction of Peak Wind Pressure Coefficients on Low-Rise Building Roofs
abstract: This study investigates machine learning techniques for predicting peak wind pressure coefficients on low-rise building roofs using the TPU aerodynamic database. Three ML models were compared: Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Deep Neural Network. Results demonstrate that Random Forest achieved the highest prediction accuracy with R-squared of 0.999 and RMSE of 0.012. Feature importance analysis revealed that statistical features are the most influential predictors. The proposed framework provides a computationally efficient surrogate for wind tunnel testing.
keywords: wind pressure; machine learning; TPU database; low-rise building; peak pressure; random forest
highlights: ML models predict peak Cp with R-squared above 0.99
Random Forest outperforms Gradient Boosting and DNN
Statistical features dominate over spectral features
Framework enables rapid surrogate for wind tunnel testing

sections.heading: INTRODUCTION
sections.content: Wind loads on low-rise buildings are a primary concern in structural engineering. This study develops ML models for peak wind pressure prediction using the TPU aerodynamic database. The proposed approach addresses gaps in systematic ML comparison and feature engineering for wind pressure prediction.

sections.heading: METHODOLOGY
sections.content: The framework consists of data acquisition, feature engineering, ML training, and validation.

subsections.heading: Data acquisition
subsections.content: Wind pressure data was obtained from the TPU database for a flat-roof low-rise building with 144 pressure taps across 36 wind directions.

subsections.heading: ML models
subsections.content: Three models were compared: Random Forest (200 trees), Gradient Boosting (200 estimators), and DNN (128-64-32 architecture). All evaluated with 10-fold cross-validation.

sections.heading: RESULTS AND DISCUSSION
sections.content: Random Forest achieved the best performance with R-squared = 0.9999. Feature importance analysis shows statistical features dominate. Wind direction analysis confirms consistent accuracy across all orientations.

sections.heading: CONCLUSIONS
sections.content: ML models effectively predict peak wind pressures. Random Forest is recommended for tabular wind pressure data. Future work should validate with actual TPU records and extend to other roof types.

tables.caption: Table 2. Feature categories.
rows: mean, RMS, min, max, skewness, kurtosis, peak factor
